7.reason: [Error: loop condition assumes newline exists; if input has no newline (e.g., buffer full) or is empty, it may cause out-of-bounds access; also does not stop at null terminator]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: loop condition assumes newline exists; if input has no newline (e.g., buffer full) or is empty, it may cause out-of-bounds access; also does not stop at null terminator]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition assumes newline is present; if input does not contain newline (e.g., from gets), loop may run past the string. Also, when i=0, s[i-1] is out of bounds, causing undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Loop condition checks for '\n', which may cause out-of-bounds access or infinite loop if input lacks a newline; should check for '\0' or use strlen. Additionally, declaring 'int i' inside the for-loop initializer may cause compilation errors in strict C89 environments.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Loop condition lacks '\0' check, risking out-of-bounds access or infinite loop if input doesn't end with a newline.

11.reason: This condition is evaluated before checking if the surrounding characters are valid and of the same type, which may lead to accessing invalid memory (e.g., when '-' is at the start or end of string) and incorrect logic ordering.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: accesses s[i+1] and s[i-1] without bounds checking; also order check should be performed only after type check; if types don't match but order holds, dash is not printed (missing else)]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This check uses s[i-1] which is invalid when i=0. Additionally, when left < right but types do not match, the dash is not printed, violating the requirement to output unchanged characters.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Accessing s[i-1] when i=0 causes out-of-bounds memory access; also s[i+1] may access beyond the string terminator if '-' is at the end.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Accesses s[i-1] when i=0 causing undefined behavior; checks ASCII order before type validation, causing '-' to be omitted when types differ but right char has higher ASCII value.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  This condition is evaluated before checking if the surrounding characters are valid and of the same type, which may lead to accessing invalid memory (e.g., when '-' is at the start or end of string) and incorrect logic ordering.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition is evaluated before checking if the surrounding characters are valid and of the same type. This can lead to accessing s[-1] when i=0, causing undefined behavior. Also, it doesn't ensure both characters are of the same category (e.g., lowercase, uppercase, digit).]

28.reason: [Missing else clause: if s[i+1] > s[i-1] but types don't match, the dash should be printed; currently it is skipped.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Missing else clause: if s[i+1] > s[i-1] but types don't match, the dash should be printed; currently it is skipped.]

13.reason: Repeated out-of-bounds access to s[i-1] and s[i+1] without boundary checks.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Repeated out-of-bounds access to s[i-1] and s[i+1] without boundary checks.

18.reason: Same out-of-bounds access issue for s[i-1] and s[i+1].

29.reason: Only handles cases where s[i+1]<=s[i-1], failing to print '-' when types mismatch but s[i+1]>s[i-1].

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Only handles cases where s[i+1]<=s[i-1], failing to print '-' when types mismatch but s[i+1]>s[i-1].
